sentences_input.text: Pesach was a very special holiday in our house because we-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] we should fall asleep.
The services were conducted and the songs were sang such a long time that most-- my younger brother and I, we always fell asleep.

But it was a very cheerful type of a holiday, with many songs and asking the four questions, which I always volunteered to be the first one to ask the-- the four questions, which are called the [YIDDISH].
And-- [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] it was a very festive holiday in our house.
My mother used to work quite a bit to cook their meals and to prepare the dishes to make them kosher for Pesach, and boil them in water in big pots.
And it was quite a-- a busy time in-- in our home.
But it was also a very special time, a lot of anticipation of Pesach, of the Seder.
And it was a-- It was a long holiday because we used to have two Seders, and then have the [HEBREW].
So it was a festive as well as a religious holiday.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] We were sitting at the table and all I remember, that took a long time for the Seders to-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] to complete the Seders.
It used to be very late at night.
So I know that my younger brother definitely, and I myself, fell sleep most of the time at the end.
We never finished it.
So-- but it was a very important holiday [INAUDIBLE] in my home.
My-- my-- well, my grandfather was a widower.
So he would eat a Pesach.
I remember he used to come to us quite often.
And he would go to his daughter's, because we were-- we had-- we lived in the building.
Two of my aunts-- other aunts lived in the building, and his son.

Unlike other holidays which were, like Rosh Hashanah, Yom Kippur, that was-- where we spent a lot of-- a long time in the temple listening to the-- to the cousin and the rabbi speaking and [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] waiting for any-- for the [NON-ENGLISH] to [INAUDIBLE] so that we-- the services would be over.
And we had a temple that was-- the men were sitting on the first floor, and the women were sitting on the second floor.
It was like a round-- round dome with balconies.
And [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] on Yom Kippur, many people would faint and-- because of the fasting.
It was a very-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] everyone was at awe, to, and-- and especially on Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur.
It was like [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] a-- I don't know how to describe it, but it was like [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] a special atmosphere.
Like in Hebrew they would say like [HEBREW], [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS] a solemn occasion.

[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] Then we also had very festive and less religious holidays, like Purim, and-- which we used to get-- we used to go and make noises in the temple with the [INAUDIBLE] and we-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] I was too small, but my brothers and my father used to get the [NON-ENGLISH], dancing with the Torah.
And-- and-- and Hanukkah was probably the most enjoyable because we had candles, lit candles every night, and-- and drank.

And we sang the various songs about Hanukkah and about the destruction of the Temple and the-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] and the, also, the [INAUDIBLE] how the Miracle of the Lights, how there's a song that describes it, how the candles were burning for eight days without-- without-- actually, not the candles, the oil was.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] But I remember these--
Yeah, I remember.
[SINGING IN HEBREW] We sang various songs.
This one I remember the best.
I would say that in my father-- grandfather's building, there mainly was-- there was Jewish people, mainly, living there.
I don't remember.
We had a caretaker which was non-Jewish.
And he had his own apartment, because of-- he was the custodian, the caretaker.

And also he used to come around on Shabbat and high holidays, and turn on and off the candles, the lights, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] and perform some work that was not allowed because of the Shabbat, of the Sabbath.
[PAUSES FOR 8 SECONDS] The family was important in Oswiecim because it was a-- an old, established family.
I-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] my grandmother [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] came from-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] her name was-- maiden name was Van [?
Plasner. ?] She-- they were old Austrian Jews with a title.
So she was a contessa, count-- a countess.
Van [?
Plasner, ?] that was her maiden name.
